title: neoacanthophora scandens
intro: Neoacanthophora scandens, commonly known as the "Red Coral Bush" or "Red Coral Plant", is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Acanthaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its ability to lower cholesterol levels, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties. The therapeutic actions of Neoacanthophora scandens include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which make it a potential treatment for various health conditions. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
side_effects: Neoacanthophora scandens can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and gastrointestinal problems. Ingesting the plant can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, while topical application may lead to skin rashes and inflammation. Prolonged exposure to the plant's toxins can also cause liver and kidney damage, as well as an increased risk of infections. Improper use of Neoacanthophora scandens can also trigger anaphylaxis, a severe and potentially life-threatening allergic reaction.
